raw_consumer_statement: I called and reported my Citi card stolen more than a month before they accepted a charge from XXXX XXXX of {$780.00} on XX/XX/year>. The merchant used the cancelled card number which they had on file from the prior months. My problem is with XXXX XXXX, not the merchant. They have been billing me this amount, when as per the Truth In Lending Act states that they can not do so, and the maximum I am responsible for is {$50.00}. I put in a dispute and received a temporary credit for the full amount., when they decided against me they reversed the temporary credit. When they initially reversed the charge, they also reduced my credit line to only the amount owed. So when they reversed the temporary credit, they reported that I went over my credit limit. Which I did not, and have not even used the new card they sent me. It went over the limit due to their lowering my credit, and then recharging me {$780.00} on the stolen card. The merchant only had the old stolen card on file, not the new card I was issued as a replacement. I have tried two times to verify when the card was reported stolen, and both times they lied and said it was on XX/XX/XXXX ( I can verify it was at least 30 days prior )
